task_completion.description: Navigate to and open the Screen Capture module
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Screen Capture' module panel is visible in the sidebar or module selector.

task_completion.description: Set the capture mode to Lightbox
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Animation mode' or capture type dropdown shows 'Lightbox' selected.

task_completion.description: Configure the lightbox grid to 5 columns and 4 rows
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Columns' input shows '5' and the 'Rows' input shows '4'.

task_completion.description: Select the Red (axial) view for capture
task_completion.visual_evidence: The view selection dropdown (e.g., 'Master view' or 'View') shows 'Red' or the Red slice node selected.

task_completion.description: Set the correct output file path
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file path input field contains '/home/ga/Documents/SlicerData/Exports/brain_montage.png'.

task_completion.description: Execute the capture and verify the output
task_completion.visual_evidence: Agent clicks the 'Capture' button OR the file browser is shown displaying the newly created 'brain_montage.png' file.

integrity.description: Agent uses the 3D Slicer GUI to configure settings
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with dropdowns, spinboxes, and buttons in the Slicer interface, not just terminal commands.

integrity.description: Agent does not use Python scripting to bypass the module interface
integrity.visual_evidence: No evidence of the Python console being used to programmatically generate the screenshot without the Screen Capture module.

integrity.description: Agent interacts with the actual 3D Slicer application
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface matches the standard 3D Slicer GUI layout and is not a text editor or mock-up.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Module: Screen Capture. Required Settings: Mode='Lightbox', Columns='5', Rows='4', View='Red' (Axial). Output Path: '/home/ga/Documents/SlicerData/Exports/brain_montage.png'.
